---
title: "Thinking with Agents"
subtitle: "AI tools for teaching and research"
page-layout: full
body-classes: landing-page
title-block-style: none
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<section class="landing-hero-split">
  <div class="hero-left">
    <h1>Thinking with<br>Agents</h1>
    <p class="hero-subtitle">AI tools for teaching and research</p>
    <p class="hero-meta">Erkmen G. Aslim &amp; Emily Beam</p>
  </div>
  <div class="hero-right">
    <h2 class="schedule-heading">Upcoming Webinars · June 2026</h2>
    <div class="schedule-item">
      <div class="schedule-date">Jun 15</div>
      <div class="schedule-title">TwA Webinar: Agentic Tools for Research</div>
      <div class="schedule-detail">10:00 AM–12:00 PM ET · Online (Teams)</div>
      <a href="https://events.teams.microsoft.com/event/88b689b0-2b31-4f1a-b04b-74873f4a7305@1c177758-4d6b-43dc-aaeb-3b9c42562967" class="slides-btn">Register →</a>
    </div>
    <div class="schedule-item">
      <div class="schedule-date">Jun 17</div>
      <div class="schedule-title">TwA Webinar: Agentic Tools for Teaching</div>
      <div class="schedule-detail">10:00 AM–12:00 PM ET · Online (Teams)</div>
      <a href="https://events.teams.microsoft.com/event/bc7022b1-63ae-470e-8c41-9c7890bdf108@1c177758-4d6b-43dc-aaeb-3b9c42562967" class="slides-btn">Register →</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>
```

::: {.landing-content}

Two free 2-hour webinars. **Come to one or both** — they are independent, standalone events. Both sessions are live on Microsoft Teams.

---

[Agentic Tools for Research · June 15]{.landing-section-title}

**Monday, June 15, 2026 · 10:00 AM–12:00 PM ET**

How AI tools are changing research workflows — and how to use them effectively.

#### What we'll cover

- The AI landscape for research: what tools can and can't do
- **Live demo**: A realistic research task done with and without AI (literature review, data exploration, or code debugging)
- How to build verification habits for AI-generated content
- Q&A

#### Who is this for?

Targeted toward faculty, researchers, and graduate students in economics and related fields — but all are welcome.

```{=html}
<div style="margin: 1.5rem 0 2.5rem;">
  <a href="https://events.teams.microsoft.com/event/88b689b0-2b31-4f1a-b04b-74873f4a7305@1c177758-4d6b-43dc-aaeb-3b9c42562967" class="slides-btn" style="font-size: 0.95rem; padding: 0.6rem 1.5rem;">
    Register for AI for Research (June 15) →
  </a>
</div>
```

[Agentic Tools for Teaching · June 17]{.landing-section-title}

**Wednesday, June 17, 2026 · 10:00 AM–12:00 PM ET**

How faculty can use AI tools for course design, student feedback, and teaching students to use AI effectively.

#### What we'll cover

- **Live demo**: AI-assisted course preparation — from rough notes to polished materials
- **Live demo**: Feedback at scale — using AI to scaffold (not automate) student feedback
- Q&A

#### Who should attend

- Faculty in economics, social sciences, or related fields
- Instructors at any level (community college through R1)
- Graduate students who teach
- Academic staff involved in curriculum design

```{=html}
<div style="margin: 1.5rem 0 2.5rem;">
  <a href="https://events.teams.microsoft.com/event/bc7022b1-63ae-470e-8c41-9c7890bdf108@1c177758-4d6b-43dc-aaeb-3b9c42562967" class="slides-btn" style="font-size: 0.95rem; padding: 0.6rem 1.5rem;">
    Register for AI for Teaching (June 17) →
  </a>
</div>
```

---

[About the presenters]{.landing-section-title}

**[Emily Beam](https://eabeam.github.io)** is an associate professor of economics at the University of Vermont, specializing in development economics and randomized impact evaluations. She uses AI tools daily across research, teaching, and project coordination.

**[Erkmen G. Aslim](https://sites.google.com/view/erkmenaslim/)** is an assistant professor of economics at the University of Vermont. His research spans health economics and applied microeconomics, and he integrates AI tools into both research and teaching workflows.

Together, they developed and delivered the [UVM Economics AI Bootcamp](past-events.qmd) (Spring 2026) and created the Thinking with Agents resource collection.

---

[New to AI tools?]{.landing-section-title}

If you've never used a terminal, Git, or an AI coding tool — start with our [Getting Started](getting-started.qmd) guide. It sequences the best existing resources into a clear path. Most economists can work through it in an afternoon.

:::
